name: Buttermilk Pecan Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Grease and flour four loaf pans.
2. Separate the eggs into yolks and whites.
3. In a bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Add the pears and pecans; stir until combined. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl, cream the butter and sugar. Add the egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in the vanilla.
5. Alternately add the dry ingredients and buttermilk to the butter mixture, mixing well after each addition.
6. With clean, dry beaters, whip the egg whites until they form firm, glossy peaks.
7. Gently fold one-third of the egg whites into the batter to lighten it. Then gently fold in the remaining whites.
8. Pour the batter evenly into the prepared pans (fill each pan about two-thirds full).
9. Reduce the oven temperature to 350°F.
10. Bake for 65 minutes, or until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Ten minutes before baking is complete, rotate the pans front-to-back so that both sides of the cakes brown evenly. Let the cakes cool on wire racks for five minutes before turning them out onto the racks to finish cooling upright.
12. Before serving, decorate the fruitcakes with whole nuts and mixed candied fruit peel.
